Name: Handbook of Hope: Theory, Measures, & Applications
Description: This comprehensive handbook provides an in-depth expiration of hope theory, measurement tools, and practical applications for various domains, making it a valuable resource for research

Name: Hope Rising: How the Science of Hope Can Change Your Life
Description: This book explores the concept of hope in the context of social change and activism, offering real-world examples of how hope can be a driving force in creating positive transformat

Name: Making Hope Happen
Description: Positive psychologist, and Gallop Senior Scientist, delves into the practical aspects of hope, emphasizing its cognitive process and offering strategies for cultivating hope in everyday life.

Name: Psychology of Hope: You Can Get Here from There
Description: This foundational work by one of the pioneers in hope research explores the physiological aspects of hope, providing insights into its nature and impact on human behavior.

Name: Sew Sisters to the Stars: How Sewing Transformed the World of Flight
Description: One of 18 seamstresses in this critical role, Jean and her co-workers dubbed their group, “The Sew Sisters,” – using machine and hand stitching to build, create and repair thermal protection flight hardware and parachutes for the space shuttle and Orion!

Name: TEDxTC - Peter Benson - Sparks: How Youth Thrive
Description: Dr. Peter L. Benson explains the Developmental Assets framework, a research-based approach to youth development that identifies the strengths and resources teens need to thrive, featuring insights from *Sparks*.

Name: “Can Hope be Changed in 90 Minutes? Testing the Efficacy of Single-Session Goal-Pursuit Intervention for College Students”
Source Name: David B. Feldman, Diane E Dreher
Description: This article by David B. Feldman and Diane E. Dreher focuses on testing if hope is malleable by utilizing a single-session 90-minute intervention to increase college students' hopeful goal-directed thinking.

Name: “Hope Theory”
Source Name: C. R. Snyder, Kevin L. Rand, & David R. Sigmon
Description: This foundational article by C.R. Snyder introduces Hope Theory, providing a theoretical framework for understanding the cognitive processes involved in hope and its implications for well-being.

Name: “Hope and Well-Being: A Decade of Research”
Description: This article by Elijah R. Murphy reviews a decade of research on hope and its connection to overall well-being, summarizing key findings and highlighting the multifaceted benefits of a hopeful mindset.
